dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Heading out? Olympus doesn't like to be kept waiting, I know. We'll remember you here, lass! Somehow it still feels as if we've only met. What an unusual time...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That it is, Achilles sir. I have these glimpses now of cherished moments I might have spent with you and the other Houseservants, had the past but gone differently. Are the others doing well?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: They seem to be. Your relatives recovered the most swiftly, but I think we're all still feeling some effects... and catching up on everything we missed, and aiding where we can.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'll miss you all. This really does feel like a dream, at times! I fear I may forget it, as one tends to do with dreams.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh. I trust you'll still remember what I always told you about fear. Please don't worry... I know we may not see you for a while, but I shall await whenever we may meet again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: That monster, truly gone... you got him, forever! Not that my faith in you wavered since your initial victory, it's just... I still can't entirely believe it. So now for Chronos, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Now for Chronos, then. One encouraging result can often spur another. And perhaps all of you shall have a brief reprieve. I'm certain you could use it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: With Prometheus and such still causing problems, we won't be getting all too comfortable just yet. Especially not while our main foe is out there still. Go get him, sunshine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: You did it, sunshine! I'd say I knew you would, but the truth is, I had an inkling of doubt... especially once I saw Typhon for myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He was quite large, I'll give him that! It's a pleasure to finally meet you face-to-face like this, Cousin. You play most beautifully!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, anything to take our minds off of what's happening. At least you bought us a bit of reprieve so I could play again at all. Typhon is going to be back, though, isn't he...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd count on that. But I am going to be back as well. For now, please bolster your defenses, and make ready for the monster's next attack. Now I had better go... my headmistress is expecting my return.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't you just contact her by Boon? I'll bring one forth for you in case that works. And thanks for everything, Cousin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: It's good to see you again, Cousin. Just as I was beginning to fear my song would be tonight's sole source of cheer!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Apollo, good to see you too. Please give my regards to the others... I would have liked to meet, but had best be getting back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Of course, you must be on your last legs. The others surely would have liked to see you too, especially your grandmother, but they couldn't leave their posts. Please accept our apologies, and let us help you to return.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Tell Lady Demeter and everyone I wish them well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: You truly think we'll soon get blasted Typhon well out of our way for good? That'd be a welcome change.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If we can rid ourselves of Chronos, then we should be able to rid ourselves of the Father of All Monsters, too. And I have not been training all my life for this only to fail now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Well! Remind me never to get on your bad side, sunshine. You ready to head home? Always good to see you, though I'd best get back to singing so that everyone stays calm.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your song is such a welcome sound after a hard night's climb, Cousin. It nearly makes me forget the troubles we're dealing with. All this light, however, I'm not used to yet...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: We're not much used to it here either for how dark it's been of late. Though let's get you back before it drains you more. Sounds like you have to pay Chronos a visit soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your song is such a welcome sound after a hard night's climb, Cousin. It nearly makes me forget the troubles we're dealing with. All this light, however, I'm not used to yet...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: We're not much used to it here either for how dark it's been of late. Though let's get you back before it drains you more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: We ought to be feasting in your name as far as I'm concerned, sunshine. But no sign of Dionysus still, and our new guest has drawn most of the attention. Soon we also won't be letting anybody in from the outside.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's all right, Cousin, as I'm not much for feasting anyhow. And we do need to find a place for this grandfather of ours. Do you know what Lord Uncle Zeus has in store for him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Not entirely, but my impression is he's sending Chronos back into the Underworld, to Lord Hades. Apparently they've reached some sort of bargain. So, you truly think our grandfather has changed?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think he has a different outlook on things, yes. As for me, sounds like I may not be able to return anytime soon. This place truly is beyond compare, and with each visit you've made me feel welcome. Thank you for that and everything.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, come on, it's all of us who should be thanking you, and quite profusely I should add. Anyhow, not like this is a permanent farewell. I'll keep in touch by Boon, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Ready to head back again? Here, let me light the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Splendid work as usual, sunshine. We're all grateful you made the trip.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: By now you'd probably much rather be where it isn't quite this bright.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: It's only thanks to you that I can sit and strum here at a time like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Wish you could stay since you just got here, though I know it's not the time. Be safe out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: It's good to see you, sunshine, but you'd best speak to Father first.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Best not to keep your Lord Uncle waiting. I'll be right here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Apollo_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I, erm, think the King and Queen might like a word?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: The storm has passed... that monster turned the weather such that not even Lord Zeus or I could stop it properly. But now he's gone, and we have you to thank.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thanks are owed all around, Grandmother. Typhon gave in to the combined might of the gods. I merely concentrated our fell strength. Now to press this advantage.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps not even Chronos can withstand what you unleashed. But be careful, Granddaughter. Make certain we shall meet again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, flower, it's truly you! Forgive me, I... we must maintain decorum here, but... I am very pleased to see you here before me, rather than within the dimness of a Boon.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Demeter, the honor is mine. I know that everyone's preoccupied contending with the siege, and it couldn't have been easy for you to be here now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Please, call me grandmother; although perhaps not here. I was furious to have missed you when you visited before, and was not about to let that reoccur. But you cannot stay can you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Unfortunately not. With Typhon dealt with for the moment, I had best be getting back. I'm so grateful to have met you like this! Shall I see you again?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_Story_01
endLines.text: If the Fates have any mercy in their hearts, then yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We did it, Grandmother! You've fought so hard for me... for all of us. But, it must be difficult, to see Chronos again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall not dignify him with so much as a passing glance, Melinoë. The Titan would have reached a different end were it for me to decide, but... I'm grateful that the outcome was not worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: From what I gathered, this may be farewell for a while. At least face-to-face! I'll still be seeing you most nights, if you'll be able to keep aiding me by Boon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Of course. And there shall come a time again when your mother shall be visiting us half the year. Perhaps then you may come along, once every few seasons at least.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_Story_01
endLines.text: Oh, and here... to keep you warm.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Grandmother, is this for me? Thank you...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We did it, Grandmother! You've fought so hard for me... for all of us. But, it must be difficult, to see Chronos again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall not dignify him with so much as a passing glance, Melinoë. The Titan would have reached a different end were it for me to decide, but... I'm grateful that the outcome was not worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: From what I gathered, this may be farewell for a while. At least face-to-face! I'll still be seeing you most nights, if you'll be able to keep aiding me by Boon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Demeter_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Of course. And there shall come a time again when your mother shall be visiting us half the year. Perhaps then you may come along, once every few seasons at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: There you are, at last! You might have kept me searching for a while yet, that you found such a secure hiding-point amid those shadows!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It sure took you long enough! I was beginning to think I might never see you again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah! That is precisely what your father said when Zagreus first brought me here! Well, are we to suffer through another round?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No... this game is too easy. Tell me another one of your tales! About the Golden Age!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps when you are older, lest your father be displeased. If you defy him, you merely get a mild reprimand. If I defy him, I get cut to little bite-sized bits, which then are sprinkled throughout Tartarus!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Haha, ew! Well if Father ever does that, I shall simply go find all the bits and put you back together again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Spoken with the generosity of youth. You are the only one around here who would say such things, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Truly? But, don't the others like you, Grandfather?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, they can never get enough of me, my girl, except... we still have had our differences. But let me not concern you with the past. The only time that matters is right now. And right now...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wish to make an offering to you, O Night. For always concealing the Crossroads where I grew up, for the Nocturnal Arms, and all the many other gifts you bestowed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I thank you for this, child. And may the Nocturnal Arms long serve you as a Keepsake of sorts from me. The Witch of the Crossroads says you wield them with skill and grace.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wished to give you something more, O Night. For caring for Sister Selene, who is a dear friend, along with your wonderful children whom I've come to know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Your family's custom is not mine, child. Nor do I wish to accept such gratitude whilst there is so much to be done before the realm is at full strength again. You have my thanks, as well as that request.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: How unusual to see you here. My great ancestor and I have much to discuss, and shall resume after you take your leave.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh... forgive my rudeness having interrupted. Shall you come visit us at the Crossroads again soon, O Night?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Sporadic visits are a possibility, especially after the House of Hades is entirely restored. Although my wish is for my children to be wholly independent of my influence.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Many mothers must feel the same way.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
endLines.text: I do not know their feelings, only mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I sense the blessings of the god of war. I have not visited with him or the Olympians since I was indisposed.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think they know that you have much to do right now, O Night. Did you use to travel to the surface very much?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: My influence extends across the realms. And I have had occasion to interact with Lord Ares and the goddess Athena, amongst others. But it is not habitual.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm certain they'll look forward to when next you meet.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
endLines.text: Some of them fear me, I should think, but yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: The notoriety of the Unseen has spread throughout the realms. As a result of your efforts, few remain who would so much as dare to stand against the Underworld now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We have many loyal Shades in every region keeping watch for us, even as we eliminate remaining traces of the threats that caused us so much harm. But there is more to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: There always is, Melinoë, Princess. Which is why it can be valuable to take such moments to reflect on how far we have come, regardless of how far we have to go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Chaos typically avoids involvement in matters concerning the Underworld or the surface realms, so this was an exception. At some point we grew closer as a family, perhaps because we learned from yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My family always seems to be on the verge of falling apart. This ordeal may have strengthened our bonds, but... sometimes I fear they shall not hold forever.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Weakened bonds may be reforged. That is the reason I am here with Chaos, to start with the eldest of my kin, and work my way down... a lengthy process, but a necessary one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: My son Charon yet tirelessly serves the realm, so far as I have seen. Soon enough, the House of Hades shall have need of him again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: From what I can gather, I think he shall welcome that. He ought to be delivering the dead, not battlefield supplies.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: He shall do what the realm requires. You claim to understand him, then? He can be difficult for some to parse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Some of my children residing in your Crossroads... we have not always interacted in the most productive way. I once thought that this failing was solely mine. But, I see the pattern frequently; a curse of sorts our families share.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Even a single child seems very difficult to rear. I could not have been an easy one, for all my moods. I scarce imagine how it must have been for you with Nemesis, or Eris...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Amongst others, yes. I was the constant in most of my children's lives, yet each has been a mystery. May Queen Persephone's relationship with you not be as tenuous as so many of mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you could visit your children more often... smooth things over...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: My children tend to need their space. But as I learned with Chaos, we have eternity to reconvene, if we so desire.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: How has my daughter Eris been? I regret that Hecate had to contend with her whilst I was unavailable. I did not think that Strife would choose to stay in any given place.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Strife. While Eris has been difficult now and again, despite our differences, we have an understanding. She's... exciting to be around, if nothing else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: That is more than I can say. Yet I have learned from her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: My many children dwell in darkness, yet I know where most of them reside. But not my daughters the Three Fates. They were cast into exile, to your knowledge?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes... Chronos sought to get them out of the way, as part of his conquest. I think he feels quite badly about it now, but that hasn't helped us find them. What can we do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: We look for signs. If the Fated List has been revealing prophecies to you, then some traces of their power to communicate remain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, perhaps, I once presided solely over all the Underworld. There came a point when this exceeded my ability, and so my daughters the Three Fates selected someone to assist with governing the realm: Your father.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That was when he split off from his own brothers and foster-sisters... who became the Olympians. It must have been a turbulent time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: It was a time of change. I did not expect for Hades to take to his role as readily, for it was difficult. Or for Persephone to bring life to this place in a welcome way. And now you and your brother... the realm is better off because of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust you are to thank for having shaken my son Hypnos from his slumber and compelled him to return. He had been doing fairly well in his responsibilities, up to a certain point.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please forgive him, O Night. He must have fallen into such a state as a defensive response when the Titan Chronos struck. I am relieved he's well... assuming that he is?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: He is himself. His brother Thanatos is pleased to have him back, as is your own. Thank you for looking after him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I had not expected to see my son Moros in your Crossroads, amongst the others. Hecate says that he delivered you the Fated List in answer to a summoning?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I sought the aid of the Three Fates, knowing their prophecy had stopped Chronos before. Lord Moros arrived in their stead, and has been staying with us since. He's very kind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I have not heard him thus described before. But, most only know him by his role. He must have worried deeply for the Fates... that pain of losing relatives.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: You and my daughter Nemesis are close, Hecate says. She must have made your training rigorous, for she has always led with strength; withholding emotion but little else.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Growing up, I thought for certain that the brunt of my task would fall to her. When it didn't, that put a strain on us, but... I think we're good now. I care for her a lot... if that's all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Whom you care for is not for me to decide. It seems your bonds grew stronger from the strain of your circumstances, then. But Nemesis shall always challenge you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: You bear my Aspect of the Black Coat. Long has it been since I last wielded it myself. I solve most conflicts indirectly now, but I do think at times about the power of Xinth.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I am deeply honored to have been entrusted with that power, O Night. Most of the conflicts I've encountered recently... only direct approaches seemed to work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand how that may be, or how it may appear. Thus far, the six Nocturnal Arms have served us well. When I myself could not safeguard the realm, they did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I have many children, but your mother the Queen, she does not. Therefore it saddens me that she was not available to witness your initial growth. Yet I am pleased that we remember a happier possibility.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We're back in one another's lives, and share some wonderful memories... I couldn't ask for much more. Queen Persephone must be fortunate to have guidance from the most experienced mother that the world has ever known!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: It is a learning process even still, though I impart what knowledge I possess. But your mother's intuition for it often is superior to mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I look forward to getting to know her better myself. Have the two of you always been close?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Always is a long time. Though, Queen Persephone and I, we have been close since prior to your brother's birth, and ever since.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Thank you for looking out for her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Many responsibilities of mine accumulated whilst the Titan had me in his temporary snare. Thus I am likely to remain unseen for some time. Until the future reunites us, thank you once again, Melinoë, Princess. And you farewell.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: You have a blessing from my faithful Eye of Night. She is dependable; a quality that I have come to prize above all else. The two of you are close?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She was always watching over me since I was very small. I am proud to call her Sister now, and to have worked with her directly in all this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: From what I gather, the Moon herself feels very much the same. Long may the two of you illuminate each other's greatest strengths.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Your service to the realm may go unseen, but has not gone unnoticed, O Melinoë, Princess. As Fear spreads, so does our influence.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: In shadow, I serve; to shadow I return, O Night. And may your will be done.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
endLines.text: If that is your own will.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: My testaments within the Oath, enacted to the very last. Despite your demonstrated skill, I was uncertain whether you had the capacity.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was uncertain whether I had the capacity either, O Night. If ever my confidence is unwavering, then there's nothing for me to prove. And nothing to strive for.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Well then, it seems you may require something new to strive for now. I trust more Fear may always spread...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: A very fearsome evening this is turning out to be. The influence of our domain spreads far beyond the Underworld, to the surface, to the heavens, and beyond.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To the heavens and beyond...? Mount Olympus seemed the very top of the world... to think there may be something past that point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: You have already been there yourself. Transcended space and time and dream; so says the Witch of the Crossroads. By my determination, you have gone quite far.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: That scythe... that must be Zorephet in the Aspect designated to my Thanatos. He may soon desire its return.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I had not considered that. Well if the time has come for Zorephet to be with Thanatos once more, then I am grateful for the time we had, and entrust it to you now, O Night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I did not mean this soon. The Underworld is not yet ready for my son to carry out his work. You are to remain bearer of the Moonstone Axe till then.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wished to thank you for the Gifts of the Veil, O Night. To have earned the title of your Champion is perhaps the greatest honor of my life.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: You possess the Gifts of the Veil. Then, that is good. How did you come about them, and who bestowed the title?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I... completed the trials you put forth, that Commander Schelemeus put forth on your behalf... unless...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh yes, the skeleton, of course. I knew him by a different title once. I trust that he administered my trials, as you call them, capably?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Well, I should think so... yes...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: We welcome you unto the solitude of the abyss, Melinoë, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: The House of Hades is beginning to grow even stronger than it always was.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Chaos imagined that you would arrive, and now you stand before us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father's House can grow quite boisterous, so I seek solace in these depths.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Your brother has been doing well, and often wonders how you fare.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos asked about you recently, as though I had not already told him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust that Eris has not been too much of an impediment of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Not long ago I had encountered Nemesis whilst here, and we each spoke of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fated List remains within your care, and you have Moros to watch over it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: May the Nocturnal Arms continue to make manifest your will and wrath.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Chaos and I have aeons of history to discuss, so please do not wait up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: This is a very fearsome night, and we have you and all your Vows to thank for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: May the blessing of faithful Selene guide you well beyond this point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Persephone the Queen is often filled with joy that you have reunited finally.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: In our obscurity lies strength. Seek to be valuable, not to be known.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I and the Origin of all Creation welcome you unto this place once more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Here it is desolate and quiet, though slightly less than usual right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: You have my gratitude for your contributions to the reconstruction of our realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: My children who have met you or remember you are better for it, O Melinoë, Princess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Your presence in the House of Hades is remembered well and rather frequently.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: My daughters have returned to us. Knowing them, they gave you the impression that they did so on their own. However, if not for your efforts with the Fated List, they might have never found their way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Each prophecy fulfilled, a signal to the Fates... a beacon in the darkness of their exile, something of the sort?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: That is a reasonable way to think of it. You have done my family a great service, O Melinoë, Princess. The depth of my gratitude is beyond my means to describe.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You honor me, O Night. May all of us cease losing family members for a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_LordHades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Nyx?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_LordHades_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx? Nyx! Answer me! Blast....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Hecate, you have done all I could have asked, and more. My wayward children here were safe within your care. And by the wisdom of your counsel, I am free.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You always have been generous, O Nyx. Above all, I am grateful you returned to us. What of your children, are they heading back with you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: They are grown, and I have much to do. If it is their wish to remain within these Crossroads, then I ask that you comply. I trust they have not been a burden?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Oh, no, of course not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Then good. I cannot say when next I shall return. Farewell.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I see you all appear to be pursuing your responsibilities with diligence. Each child of mine, a valuable asset to the Witch of the Crossroads in my absence.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Whatever she says goes, Mother. We did what we could.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: And Hypnos, he was here throughout this whole ordeal as well. Be certain to pay due respect to Hecate. You may remain here for the time, so long as more is to be done, and that you all continue to cooperate. Are we understood?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Of course, Mother. We cooperate here all the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Of that, I am now certain. But I cannot remain to see it with my own eyes, for the realm has need of me below. Farewell, and may darkness guide you where moonlight cannot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm... is my hair getting lighter and my features getting fairer, or did I consume something that really isn't sitting well...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Prince Zagreus, please listen to my voice! Can you hear me? Can you hear me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Definitely something I consumed, then. Or else... oh this is just a dream...! Hypnos has been growing rather bold with these! Is that you in there, mate?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, I... look, yes, technically this is a dream. But it's the only way I could reach out to you across the span of Time! Now, please! Our family is in danger, and I need your aid!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, all right, slow down! It's fortunate I rather like such realistic dreams. And what do you mean our family? Who are you? Probably one of Mother's many cousins. Wouldn't explain the eyes, though. Or my father's sigil on your outfit...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Let me speak! Look, this is going to be difficult for you to accept, but... I'm Melinoë... your sister. From another time; a future time, not all that distant from your own!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...See, that was going to be my next guess. Well, dream-sister-from-a-future-time? I would never want to snap awake before hearing you out, so what's the situation? What do you want me to do...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I want you to find our grandfather Chronos, and destroy him! Because there's only so much I can do here myself. He's taken over your entire House...! Imprisoned our father, Mother, you, everybody...! And now he threatens Olympus. All as vengeance for what the gods once did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Right. When you say what the gods once did, you mean the part where they got rid of Chronos permanently, long before I was ever born? Or you for that matter. But in the not-all-that-distant future, he returns, and single-handedly takes over everything...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes. Please believe me! I myself was very little when the Titan struck, and have been training all my life in preparation for this very moment! But I cannot stop Chronos on my own. Will you aid me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm guessing not? Since this is a dream, and I'll likely just forget about it soon as I wake up! Also, you look and sound just like a figment of my imagination would probably look and sound. Can you prove anything you've said?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You won't forget me when you wake, I promise you. As for the proof you seek... you must know of Hecate, our mother's handmaiden! She trained me for this. Seek her, and tell her what I've said — but no one else!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Sister, I'm afraid our mother has no such handmaiden... although, in some strange way you mentioning a name I've never heard is better proof than anything you've said to me thus far. Wait, what's the matter...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's Chronos! I've incapacitated him, but he'll return... just as he will eventually in your own time. I need to go... before he finds me here, I need to go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: But you just got here! Look... Melinoë, was it? Will I ever see you again, or was this more of a one-off?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I found you across dreams and time, Brother. I'll stop at nothing to do it again! Until then, please, let this remain a secret between us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Great, I can't even tell Hypnos. Well, suit yourself! See you another day, Sister. Or night...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Just as I'm about to get my hair under control! Who are you, and what have you done with my reflection...? Only joking. I remembered everything just as you said. Particularly since you're here again so soon. Where were we?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was trying to persuade you to take me at my word that I'm your sister from a future time, in which the Titan Chronos has usurped the House of Hades. I mean to stop him, and require your assistance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: I have a wide variety of questions first, even knowing that our time apparently is limited. First off, why me? Although I am the designated Security Specialist around here, so perhaps I know the answer to that one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Some Security Specialist. Chronos caught the House entirely off guard! But you're my closest kin, you've subverted the will of the Fates before, and slain gods, if not Titans... I also lack the means of reaching anybody else this way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, great! I feel that strong kinship between us already. Next question: Where even is this Chronos, and what would you have me do if and when I locate him? A two-parter for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't know where Chronos is exactly, in your time, though I do have a lead: His remains were scattered to the deepest pits of Tartarus, which means he's probably recovering below where you reside. As for what to do if you can locate him... nothing as yet. We'll get to that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, I hate to break it to you, Melinoë, but this isn't all that comprehensive a plan. Especially for something you've been planning all your life. Do I just wait for you to work through the details, or...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No! Look. You're Security Specialist! Begin an investigation, though in absolute secrecy. Is anything amiss anywhere in the House, or in Tartarus? Any unusual activity from Satyrs, for example?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: We've had our share of Satyr problems, though not anywhere nearby. But I can have a look around. What about you...? Wait, are you in my bedchambers?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What's left of them, yes! I can only reach you like this while we're in the same location across Time. At any rate... I have to find the means for you to destroy the Titan there before he regains his full strength. But... blast.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: What is it, do you have to go again? Then don't wait up on my account. I'll see what I can do and speak to you when next you haunt my dreams. Take care, Sister! We'll sort this out.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You make it sound so effortless... farewell, Brother. Until we see each other next.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Hi there, Melinoë! My investigation into our grandfather's scheming has begun, though difficult to make good progress without telling anybody what I'm up to. Don't take this the wrong way, but... this truly is your only idea?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes. I don't think I can stop Chronos here. Even Olympus is stuck trying to keep his forces at bay. And he's far too hateful and hard-headed to stop on his own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: That's our family for you. Father certainly has no love for Chronos but he never speaks of him. Do you... happen to know what went over between them, to have made things this bad?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're in a better position to ask our father about that than I am. Chronos was a tyrant, and his children wouldn't have it! The Fates themselves deemed that the gods should rule! But that apparently wasn't enough for him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: So now you want me to stop him for you. I mean, for us. I've had mixed experiences vanquishing certain members of our family before, but... that wasn't forever. And you've said next to nothing about how you expect me to do it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Look... I have knowledge of an incantation known as Dissolution of Time. It requires several complicated steps I shall explain along the way. If it works... it should unravel him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Unravel him. That's a little vague for me, unless Chronos turns out to be a spool of yarn. Can you be more specific?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Unmake him. Destroy his ambitions along with him. Undo all the harm he caused. A threefold family bond is required: you, me, and Chronos. Then we shall infuse a special weapon with the negation of his very being... I'll get into that later.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Well great I think you cleared everything up. Though I'll admit I don't like having to kill off family members just because Father doesn't know how to get along with them.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You would blame Father in all this? Ungh, blast... Chronos is starting to revive. I have to go. Keep searching for any sign of him! And remember, this is for all of us!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: I will, and... I understand, I think. We didn't ask to be born into this but it's what we got. We'll handle it as best we can.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's right. Thank you, Brother. Take care of yourself... and be kind to Mother, Father, Cerberus, and all, for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Hello again, Sister. You wanted me to search for signs of Chronos preparing for a big surprise, and... while it isn't much... I did locate machinery in Tartarus that seemed a little out-of-place.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Machinery... you mean the gears that turn the ever-shifting chambers. In my time, that machinery has spread through Tartarus as far as I can see. What do you make of it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Well... normally I wouldn't have made anything of it, but perhaps it's connected to what you're saying. I'm working on tracing where it leads. What about you, any word about how we ought to proceed?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We need a weapon. The problem is that Chronos can resist the Arms once used to vanquish him. On top of that, it needs to be available both to you in your time, and to me in mine. The closer to our family the better. Can you think of something suitable?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Nothing immediately comes to mind. I have some mortal weapons in my chambers but they hardly worked at any point. A weapon both in my time and in yours... and none of the Infernal Arms will do...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Give it some thought, but please make haste. Chronos shall realize what I am up to here eventually, and this connection weakens every night. For now I'd better go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Hold on! What happened to the servants of this House... to my friends? Loves of my life? You said Chronos took everybody. Did you mean everybody-everybody, or...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well... no, erm, Hypnos is with us, but fast asleep. Commander Schelemeus, too; he's marshaling the Shades still loyal to our father. Poor Cerberus has been cast out into the Mourning Fields. But as for all the rest...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, at least the good Commander Schelemeus made it out. But Nyx, Achilles, Thanatos, Megaera, Dusa, Orpheus... our grandfather has all of them locked up?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He has our father bound in chains in Tartarus. I understand he's trapped the others... frozen them in Time. But we can break his hold on them. I'm certain that we can. Goodbye for now, Brother! Think of a weapon we can use to set this right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Gigaros. My father's spear. You said that we would need a weapon that's available in both our times, is close to our family, and not been used to slay Chronos before. That one should do. It's big... and it hurts.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Huh... I see how that may work. Unfortunately, I've no idea where to find this Gigaros; Chronos may have gotten hold of it. But that's in my time... while in yours...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, Father doesn't often let me use it anyway, and it's sealed off in his chambers. There must be something else. Though, at least I found the place where Chronos probably is piecing himself back together little by little!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You did? Where, what happened? How do you know it's the correct location?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Well for one thing, it appears to be directly underneath this House! Odd place to bury one's predecessor, but Father can be quite particular with punishments. Anyway when I descended deeper into Tartarus, I found a Satyr infestation that led me to some sort of cave. I went no farther for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Good. And do not enter until everything's prepared. But let's go back a moment to our father's spear. Is there some way you could procure it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure. Father keeps it locked away, though no Security Specialist of any worth would be so easily deterred! I'm risking an eternity of torment, but sounds like Chronos has that in store for me regardless. So... what shall I do with Gigaros assuming I can get my hands on it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Leave it here in your chambers for me to find. Could you persuade our father to give it to you, without letting him know exactly why? If we tamper too much with Time, Chronos shall find out. I don't need its physical form, only its essence sent across our astral-psychic gateway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Details, details, Sister! I like unreasonable challenges, and Father owes me anyway. So are you some sort of oracle, gathering up ancient artifacts, speaking to me in dreams and such? Perhaps these count as nightmares, what with the terrible future to look forward to.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We'll change that future. And I'm no oracle; I'm what you call a Witch. We're getting closer to our goal, Brother. Claim Father's spear, and I have more to do myself. I'll see you again soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Good news, Sister! It took some doing but I managed to persuade our father to let me hang onto his favored spear a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You did? That's great! But, where is it? I found no trace of any such weapon when I passed through here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I have it secure, except... I felt a little hesitant about handing it over. Or however you mean to get hold of it across this astral-psychic-whatever-you-call-it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus... I need that spear. We need that spear. What misgivings could you possibly be having now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Melinoë... you're asking me to permanently slay a member of our family whom I've never even met. I know it's important... but that doesn't mean I want to go through with it. I don't even know if I can.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Of course you can! What do you mean, you don't want to go through with it? We're the children of the Underworld King and Queen! We do what is required of us! Not merely what we desire.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: You sound just like Father. There was a time I never could communicate with him either and I sorely wished that he was dead and gone. I'm glad I never had the capability... I might have used it then.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I sound just like Father? Good! Yet there you go, comparing him to Chronos once again. Just because they're related doesn't make them anything alike. Nor is this situation remotely similar to the one you were in!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I don't know! My situation also involved family members desperately trying to kill each other!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We're trying to prevent that! Ungh, blast. We'll discuss this more another night. I can't force you to cooperate with me, but I can haunt your dreams!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Well it's a good thing I'm susceptible to threats! This was a lovely chat. See you some other nap-time, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Count on it! And mark you my words, we're going to fix this situation! We're not going to let our family fall apart!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh hi, Sister. Back to haunt my dreams some more? I didn't like where we left off, arguing over our relatives and our duty. Besides, Father always says no fighting in this House.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I didn't feel good about it either, and... I couldn't help thinking about what you said. I know you were being honest with me about your concerns. All this must be so much to take in.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Believe me I'm absorbing it as quickly as I can. We have this sinister grandfather who's resorted to all-out war against the rest of the family! One way or another... he needs to be stopped.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's right. And I've been trained in how to stop him, though unfortunately it requires you to do some of the dirty-work. The worst of it, in fact. I... can understand why you would be reluctant.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: So what do we do? Say we proceed as planned. I get Gigaros to you, you do something to it, I use it to destroy Chronos in my time before he takes control in yours... then, everything would be all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well I assume so, yes! We can't anticipate exactly what shall happen in a world without Time, but it's existed in that state before... and can't be worse than letting him prevail.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: But if Chronos is gone... what happens to you? You're here because of everything he did.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No! If Chronos hadn't taken Father's throne, we'd be together as a family! He denied me a father, a mother, a three-headed dog... you! And some evening he'll deny you that and more if we don't act.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: OK! OK. It's just... it turns out I have a sister, and I don't want to lose her over this is all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't want to lose you either, Zagreus. I want you back. You and all the rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Well then... I... better get you Father's spear. I'll have it for you next we meet. I promise.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thanks, Brother. Leave Gigaros in your chambers for me, and we'll go from there. For all our sakes.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, you did it...! I found the traces of Gigaros here, which should be what I need for what comes next.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Right. So, now I wait for you to contact me again, once you've infused our father's spear with some temporal nightmare curse, and... I'll descend into Grandfather's fetid lair where I'll drive said spear into his heart? Something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Whatever it takes. But... I've been giving this a lot of thought, and... there's an alternative I wanted to propose. You said before you've never even met Chronos. Let's say you find him in his fetid lair. What are the outcomes then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, let's see. One, I could murder him as we discussed. Two, I could fail miserably in the attempt. Or three, we hit it off, have a rousing conversation, and become best mates?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My thoughts as well, more or less. Bear with me, but... what if, prior to striking, you first took that latter course? Got to know him. Endeared yourself to him, if possible.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, what? We'd have ourselves a nice grandson-to-grandfather chat? I could invite him to the House for a bottle of Ambrosia! He's been festering awhile; perhaps he's keen for company. You're serious.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I am, but... he's been festering for far too long... and very bitter that our family broke ties with him. His ambitions likely can't be changed, even in your time. Though if nothing else, perhaps you can use such knowledge against him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You really do mean he and I should have a chat. So we become best mates, what then? Oh... huh. He wouldn't take over the House. He wouldn't have to! But, Father would absolutely lose his mind if he sees him again...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Take Chronos in as prisoner! As Security Specialist, it ought to be within your right. He probably is far too proud to go quietly, but... it's a chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, why not? He hasn't met me, right? Or you for that matter. And he can't be harder to talk to than Father. I like this plan, Sister! More than the kill-on-sight version, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Good. But I need assurances that if the conversation does not go the way you wish, you will not hesitate to run him through.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Then, here are those assurances: If I meet him, can't get through to him, don't like him, and determine he's a threat to you and all of us... I'll kill him. Then and there.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I believe you. All my life, I had this task... Death to Chronos. But that was never the goal in its entirety... moreso the means to stop him, and for retribution. If there's another way to set this right... it's worth a shot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Agreed. Well, then... I'd better brush up on my spearwork just in case. Besides, the sooner I wear myself out, the sooner I'll be able to get right back to sleep and see you again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Be ready. Preparing Gigaros may take a little while, and... I may have difficulty reaching you until such time. But I'll return. Till then, be well, Zagreus. Big brother.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Melinoë! You've returned. You've no idea how much difficulty I had falling asleep so I could see you here again. So what's the word, have you prepared the spear?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Take hold of Gigaros, and we'll find out. You'll have to go immediately to where Chronos resides. Speak to him, but do not mention me at all, as I've not yet been born. I'll be counting on you to succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: And the plan is... once I find him... either we become best mates, or else it's Death to Chronos like you said. Do you... do you truly think there's any possibility he comes around? That I won't have to kill him?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Knowing there are infinite possibilities... then I suppose so, yes. But I'm so angry with him still, and frightened about what may come to pass. He's taken so much from me, from all of us, and yet... he wants his family back, too. One way or another, he'll get what's coming. We all will.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...All right. But we're getting your family back, not just his! Whew, this is a lot. To think this spear of Father's has the power to destroy a deathless Titan.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It shall in just a moment, so hold it close. May Earth and Night and Darkness hear me now: Bring true death to the deathless by my vow. Dissolution of Time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Gigaros... it's grown hot to the touch... but feels much lighter than before as well! I suppose I should be careful not to prod somebody with it... besides Chronos.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Go, Zagreus. I've done everything I can here. And we'll see each other again. Some other time than this, perhaps, but still. For now, farewell.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Who goes there?! You lack the clopping of the Satyr's hooves, and have a countenance which I know not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: You must be Grandfather. I don't believe we've had the pleasure, I'm called Zagreus. I got a tip about your quite-suspicious cavern and decided to go take a look. What are you doing here, if I may ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Recuperating! What else would I possibly be doing in a place such as this? So I suppose Hades is to thank for this new grandchild of mine. I never thought that I would see the age when he became a father for himself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, he's a wonderful father, if you must know! My sole disappointment is that I'm an only child. But he and my dear mother Persephone the Underworld Queen truly love each other, so... down the line, one can hope!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Why are you telling me all this? Answer immediately, lest my Satyrs tear you to shreds! Where are those blasted Satyrs anyhow...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Grandfather... I'm telling you all this because I thought I'd make a little small talk here before I have to do you in. If you would bring pain and suffering upon my House, as our designated Security Specialist, I cannot allow that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Pah! What would you know of my intent? You dare speak to me of pain and suffering? Here I have rotted since an aeon prior to when you were but a twinkle in your father's eye! He and his brothers, traitors and usurpers!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: They're your children... Father and I also used to fight. He'd grown isolated from Olympus, and Persephone, and me. It didn't do him any good. What about you? We've only met! Are you prepared to sweep me into your old grudge?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: If necessary, yes, of course! Your father and his brothers surely have already poisoned you and your entire generation against me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Would I be speaking to you if that were the case? Here's the arrangement: Either come along quietly to a little surprise family reunion... or take this spear repeatedly. And you won't soon recuperate, I swear. Which is it going to be?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You, who threaten me with annihilation, have not experienced a world without Time! You would come to dearly miss my influence! Nor shall I be shamed and ridiculed before my kin... what would you have me do, when they look upon me?!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I don't know, perhaps just act like you're my grandfather, rather than sneaking underneath the House in some sort of profane chamber I ought have known about? You're Time. Why don't you extrapolate a bit?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Extrapolate what... a joy-filled life in which Grandfather Time is there to coddle the spoiled child of my rancorous son? Hades shall drive that accursed spear through me on sight if you do not!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: No, because you'll be under my protection. And also, no, it won't just be a joy-filled life, especially if you keep on. But it'll probably be better than whatever this is! Now make your choice already. I don't have all day or night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: <Scoff> So then, a future with such grandchildren as you, or the eternal void... how can I possibly decide...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Sister! I know you might be ready to proceed with whatever you plan to do with Gigaros, but... I'm not. After I left it for you, I... started feeling some regrets.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All this must be so much to take in... I've been giving this a lot of thought, and... there's an alternative I wanted to propose. You said before you've never even met Chronos. Let's say you find him in his fetid lair. What are the outcomes then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, let's see. One, I could murder him as we discussed. Two, I could fail miserably in the attempt. Or three, we hit it off, have a rousing conversation, and become best mates?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My thoughts as well, more or less. Bear with me but, what if... you could become best mates, as you say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, what? We'd have ourselves a nice grandson-to-grandfather chat? I could invite him to the House for a bottle of Ambrosia! He's been festering awhile; perhaps he's keen for company. You're serious.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I am, but... he's been festering for far too long... and very bitter that our family broke ties with him. His ambitions likely can't be changed, even in your time. Though if nothing else, perhaps you can use such knowledge against him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You really do mean he and I should have a chat. So we become best mates, what then? Oh... huh. He wouldn't take over the House. He wouldn't have to. But, Father would absolutely lose his mind if he sees him again...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Take Chronos in as prisoner! As Security Specialist, it ought to be within your right. He probably is far too proud to go quietly, but... it's a chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, why not? He hasn't met me, right? Or you for that matter. And he can't be harder to talk to than Father. I like this plan, Sister! More than the kill-on-sight version, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Good. But I need assurances that if the conversation does not go the way you wish, you will not hesitate to run him through.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Then, here are those assurances: If I meet him, can't get through to him, don't like him, and determine he's a threat to you and all of us... I'll kill him. Then and there.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I believe you. All my life, I had this task... Death to Chronos. But that was never the goal... only the means. If there's another way that works for all of us... it's worth a shot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: And the plan is... once I find him... either we become best mates... or else it's Death to Chronos like you said. Do you... do you truly think there's any possibility he comes around? That I won't have to kill him?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Knowing there are infinite possibilities... then I suppose so, yes. But I'm so angry with him still, and frightened about what may come to pass. He's taken so much from me, from all of us, and yet... he wants his family back, too. So go to him. One way or another, he'll get what's coming. We all will.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...All right. But we're getting your family back, not just his! Whew, this is a lot. To think this spear of Father's has the power to destroy a deathless Titan.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It shall in just a moment, so hold it close. May Earth and Night and Darkness hear me now: Bring true death to the deathless by my vow. Dissolution of Time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Gigaros... it's grown hot to the touch... but feels much lighter than before as well! I suppose I should be careful not to prod somebody with it... besides Chronos.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Go, Zagreus. I've done everything I can here. And we'll see each other again. Some other time than this, perhaps, but still. For now, farewell.

dialogueLines.text: Extrapolate what... a joy-filled life in which Grandfather Time is there to coddle the spoiled child of my rancorous son? Hades shall drive that accursed spear through me on sight if you do not!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: No, because you'll be under my protection. And also, no, it won't just be a joy-filled life, especially if you keep on. But it'll probably be better than whatever this is! Now make your choice already. I don't have all day or night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: <Scoff> So then, a future with such grandchildren as you, or the eternal void... how can I possibly decide...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Is that you, Melinoë? I can hardly see you anymore. Blast...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, can you hear me? Answer me! Zagreus!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I don't know what's going on with you, but I'm prepared to do my part as soon as you're prepared.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's no use. He can't hear me anymore. I have an incantation to complete, and mustn't remain here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Is that...? That's her, I think. Melinoë? Is that you? Can you hear me?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can hear you, but can you hear me? Hello, Zagreus? Hello?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Blast. There has to be a more dependable means of communicating across dreams and time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This is pointless. Until my incantation is complete, I don't think I'll get through to him again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Melinoë, where are you? It's me your brother! I'm just... standing around, apparently in a dream, waiting for you to show up... anytime?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Just hold on a little while longer, Brother...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: What if something happened to her... but then I wouldn't have this dream again, would I? Unless I'm not. And I'm just standing around, staring at a mirror...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He senses me but I'm unable to get through... just, wait a little while longer, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Am I really going to do this? Destroy our grandfather... surely it's what Father would want, and this sister of mine, too, but... eh, where is she anyway? Melinoë? Sister?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm here, it's just... we can't communicate like this at all, until... I have to rebuild our connection.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Hm, what to do... what's keeping her? Is she all right? Hello...? Are you all right out there?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I am, it's just... I need a bit more time! Just don't lose hope, and... be ready.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_Past_01
dialogueLines.text: Keep having this odd dream... or nightmare, I don't know. Not heard from her in quite a while...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'll be back for you, Brother, I swear... just not tonight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, I think we can fix up the rest of this place on our own. Thank you for all your help. Especially the part where you liberated us from our temporal prisons and whatnot!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Believe me, I had nothing better to do! Anyhow, we'll have to find a way to cross paths out there. And each keep an eye on Chronos as we tie up some loose ends.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_01
dialogueLines.text: It must be hard to even look at him after everything he did... I can't help but be glad we're all here, but that's easy for me to say, not having been locked in a lifelong conflict...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'll manage. You helped me realize there even was a path toward this outcome. As I faced Chronos in this House... I started to sense that being cut off from this family was consuming him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_01
dialogueLines.text: It might have consumed him completely. Good thing we make a decent brother-sister team! Past and present, real and imagined. And now we don't even need dream-time-witch-magick to stay in touch!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, I suppose not! Feels like we've been through thick and thin together, doesn't it? Even without the memories of a wholly different and generally more-pleasant life. Right now, I'd simply settle for a calmer one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, you're not the settling sort. Anyway I know this isn't goodbye, and we'll be seeing each other... though, still... I'll miss you, Little Sister. And do send Hypnos back if he can't wake up on his own.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He'll wake up. I'm going to miss you too, Big Brother. Take care of everyone, all right?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Zagreus_01
endLines.text: I will. You take care, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zeus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, my good niece, you were extraordinary, truly! So far as we can tell, no trace of that monstrosity remains! The deed is done! Typhon is dead!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: My Lord Husband has been premature to declare victory before, so I shall not believe a word of this until I hear it from your lips; perhaps not even then!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Father of All Monsters is undone... not merely dead, but eradicated, using the power of the Titan of Time, and a bit of assistance from Father's favored spear, Gigaros.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zeus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: You hear that, Hera dear? I told you young Melinoë was certain to prevail on our behalf! Our family always triumphs when we stand together with a common purpose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I remain utterly in shock. Well now, girl, if you have truly quashed that monstrous threat, then do prepare yourself for all of us to claim we never doubted you at any point. My Lord Husband is already starting.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you had not believed in me, Queen Hera, then perhaps Typhon would have been here in my stead. So thank you all... and now an even greater victory is within reach.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zeus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: The Titan Chronos... you mean to likewise destroy him using the methods you employed this very night?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If necessary. Though this power that you witnessed was for Typhon alone, and likewise what we plan for Chronos is fit only for him. The deathless cannot be destroyed unless they threaten the very fabric of the weavings of the Fates.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: No time for feasting till the other deed is done, then, yes? That's fine. The Titan's forces may grow far more desperate now, a possibility for which we ought prepare. We shall lend you all the strength we can for your own final assault.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zeus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome, young lady, to our family's glorious abode! You have our thanks for seeing to it that the monster Typhon failed to reach us here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: And you have our regrets that my Lord Husband left you to confront that horrid creature on your own. Most of the others are still fortifying the Palace; forgive our rudeness.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There's nothing to forgive, Queen Hera. I am honored to have met you and King Zeus face to face, and seen the glory of Olympus firsthand. I owe it to your generous support these many nights.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zeus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Come now, Melinoë, we have a shared objective after all! And so long as Chronos sits upon your father's throne, that objective remains incomplete.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Hurrying her off when she only just arrived, Husband? Although we've little here for entertainment other than Apollo's plucking. At least our enemies shall be back at our gates before long!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't think I can remain anyhow. I need to consult with the Witch of the Crossroads; she may have thoughts on how to rid ourselves of both our great foes at once.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zeus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: We shall find a means, of that I have no doubt! And until then, we shall be better prepared to meet their charge.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Go on, my dear. We're unaccustomed to expressing gratitude as you can plainly see. Until such time as your Lord Father and his own Queen are restored, there's little cause for cheer, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Zeus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome, young lady, to our family's glorious abode! You have our thanks for seeing to it that the monster Typhon failed to reach us here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Pleased to make your acquaintance finally, my dear! And you have our regrets that my Lord Husband left you to confront that horrid creature on your own. Most of the others are still fortifying the Palace; forgive our rudeness.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There's nothing to forgive, Queen Hera. I am honored to have met you and King Zeus face to face, and seen the glory of Olympus firsthand. I owe it to your generous support these many nights.
